data.title: Fed Policymakers Lean Towards 'Two-Way' Approach for Interest Rate Decisions
data.description: The latest Federal Reserve meeting minutes reveal a growing consensus among policymakers in favor of a 'two-way' approach to future interest rate decisions. According to NS3.AI, this shift indicates a balanced consideration of both potential rate hikes and cuts, reflecting the current economic uncertainties. The minutes highlight the importance of flexibility in monetary policy to address evolving economic conditions effectively. This approach aims to ensure that the Fed can respond appropriately to changes in inflation and employment metrics, maintaining economic stability.

data.title: Iran Submits Revised Proposal for US-Iran Negotiations
data.description: White House Press Secretary Levitt announced that Iran has submitted a new, more concise proposal that could potentially serve as a basis for negotiations between the United States and Iran. According to NS3.AI, Levitt noted that Iran's initial 10-point proposal was rejected, but the revised plan may align more closely with the US's 15-point plan. Levitt emphasized that U.S. President Donald Trump's red line remains unchanged, requiring Iran to cease uranium enrichment.

data.title: France Maintains Deficit Target Amid Middle East Uncertainty
data.description: France's Budget Minister, David Amiel, stated that despite a larger-than-expected reduction in the deficit last year, it is too early to lower the deficit target. According to Jin10, Amiel addressed the Senate Finance Committee on Wednesday, noting that the deficit decreased from 5.8% in 2024 to 5.1% in 2025, partly due to one-time factors. He highlighted significant uncertainty regarding the macroeconomic impact of the ongoing conflict involving Iran in 2026. Amiel expressed a desire to achieve better results than the 5% target outlined in budget documents, provided economic conditions allow. However, given the current uncertainty in the Middle East, the target remains unchanged. French ministers plan to update economic and fiscal forecasts when submitting the fiscal plan to the EU on April 21. Economy and Finance Minister Bruno Le Maire, speaking alongside Amiel, emphasized that it is too early to determine how the rapidly evolving conflict will affect economic activity.

data.title: Iranian Parliament Speaker: Three key terms were violated before peace talks even began.
data.description: The Speaker of the Iranian Parliament issued a statement saying that three key clauses of the 10-point proposal had been violated before negotiations even began. These include: 1. Failure to comply with the clause in the 10-point proposal regarding a ceasefire in Lebanon—which the Pakistani Prime Minister explicitly mentioned and declared to be a "comprehensive ceasefire effective immediately in all places, including Lebanon and other regions"; 2. The intrusion of a drone into Iranian airspace, which was destroyed in Lar, Fars province, clearly violating the clause prohibiting any further violations of Iranian airspace; and 3. Denial of Iran's right to enrich uranium, as included in Article 6 of the framework. Now, the "workable conditions" that should have served as the basis for negotiations have been blatantly and explicitly violated, even before negotiations began. Under these circumstances, implementing a bilateral ceasefire or conducting negotiations is unreasonable. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Federal Reserve meeting minutes: Policymakers focus on the dual risks of war with Iran
data.description: Following the outbreak of war with Iran, Federal Reserve officials weighed different scenarios facing the U.S. economy, including those requiring interest rate cuts and those potentially requiring rate hikes. The minutes of the March FOMC meeting, released Wednesday, showed that most officials were concerned that the war could impact the labor market, necessitating lower interest rates. At the same time, many officials also emphasized the risks of inflation, which could ultimately require a rate hike. The minutes indicated that a growing number of officials recommended including wording in the post-meeting statement that mentioned the possibility of a rate hike under certain conditions. The minutes stated: "Some participants believed that there were good reasons to include a two-way description of future interest rate decisions in the post-meeting statement to reflect that raising the target range for the interest rate might be appropriate if inflation persists above the target level." Following the March meeting, several Fed policymakers have indicated a preference for keeping interest rates unchanged while assessing the impact of the war. Overall, policymakers' response to the war reflects their concern about the risks associated with their dual mandate. The minutes stated, "The vast majority of participants believed that both upside risks to inflation and downside risks to employment remained high, and most participants noted that these risks had increased with developments in the Middle East." At the March meeting, Federal Reserve officials maintained the benchmark policy rate in the range of 3.5% to 3.75%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Officials Highlight Increased Economic Risks Due to Middle East Situation
data.description: The Federal Reserve's meeting minutes reveal that most attending officials believe the evolving situation in the Middle East has heightened economic risks. According to ChainCatcher, this assessment was shared during discussions among the officials. The geopolitical tensions in the region are seen as a potential factor influencing economic stability.

data.title: Federal Reserve Officials Consider Rate Hike Due to Oil Impact on Inflation
data.description: The Federal Reserve's meeting minutes reveal that several officials believe an oil shock could lead to further inflation, providing justification for a potential interest rate hike. According to ChainCatcher, this sentiment reflects concerns about rising oil prices contributing to inflationary pressures, which may necessitate monetary policy adjustments.

data.title: Federal Reserve meeting minutes: More officials mentioned the possibility of interest rate hikes
data.description: The minutes of the Federal Reserve's March meeting revealed an increase in the number of officials who wanted to signal to the market that the next step might be a rate hike rather than further rate cuts. The minutes stated, "Some participants believed there were sufficient grounds to provide a two-way description of the Committee's future interest rate decisions in the post-meeting statement." In contrast, the January meeting minutes only mentioned "a few" participants holding this view. In the Fed's terminology, "some" refers to more people than "several." (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve meeting minutes: The vast majority of participants noted that inflation is likely to move slower than expected toward the 2% target.
data.description: Federal Reserve meeting minutes: The vast majority of participants noted that inflation's progress toward the 2% target may be slower than expected, while also recognizing an increased risk of inflation persisting above target. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve meeting minutes: Several participants further postponed their assessment of the timing of interest rate cuts to the more distant future.
data.description: Federal Reserve meeting minutes: Several participants further postponed their assessment of the timing of interest rate cuts to the more distant future. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed Officials Consider War-Induced Economic Risks as Potential Reason for Future Rate Cuts
data.description: The Federal Reserve's meeting minutes reveal that a majority of officials believe the economic risks posed by ongoing conflicts could justify future interest rate cuts. According to Jin10, the officials discussed the potential impact of geopolitical tensions on the U.S. economy, noting that such risks might necessitate adjustments to monetary policy. The meeting highlighted concerns about how prolonged conflicts could affect economic stability and growth, prompting considerations for easing interest rates to mitigate adverse effects. The discussions underscore the Fed's vigilance in monitoring global events and their implications for domestic economic conditions.

data.title: Azerbaijan's BTC Oil Exports Expected to Decline in May
data.description: Azerbaijan is projected to export 15.67 million barrels of BTC crude oil through the Ceyhan port in May, according to Jin10. This marks a decrease from the 16.38 million barrels exported in April. The reduction in export volume may impact regional oil supply dynamics and market prices.

data.title: White House: Vance will lead a delegation to Islamabad this weekend for negotiations on the Iran issue.
data.description: The White House announced that Vice President Vance will lead a negotiating team to talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, this weekend. White House Press Secretary Levitt stated on Wednesday that Vance will participate in the Iran negotiations alongside US special envoys Witkov and Jared Kushner. "The first round of talks will be held on Saturday morning local time, and we look forward to these face-to-face meetings," Levitt said. (Jinshi)

data.title: Amazon's Prime Video Ultra Price Increase Gains Wall Street Support
data.description:
Amazon shares experienced an uptick on Wednesday following Wall Street's endorsement of a price increase for the United States Prime Video service. According to NS3.AI, the ad-free subscription will see a rise from $2.99 to $4.99 per month. The service is set to be rebranded as Prime Video Ultra on April 10, with an additional option for a $45.99 annual plan.

JPMorgan analyst Anmuth has adjusted his target price for Amazon (AMZN) shares, raising it from $265 to $280. Meanwhile, BofA Securities maintained its target at $275, suggesting that the price adjustment could bolster Amazon's revenue by 2026.

data.title: San Francisco Fed President Daly: U.S. Economy Remains Strong Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: San Francisco Federal Reserve President Mary Daly stated on Wednesday that despite the uncertainties and volatility caused by the Iran conflict, the fundamentals of the U.S. economy remain strong. According to Jin10, Daly noted that consumer spending and business investments are ongoing. She acknowledged concerns about potential inflationary pressures, emphasizing that it is the Fed's responsibility to monitor these developments. Additionally, she addressed worries about the labor market's stability, asserting that it is currently maintaining a solid level.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly: The fundamentals of the US economy remain sound.
data.description: San Francisco Federal Reserve President Mary Daly said on Wednesday that despite the uncertainty and volatility brought about by the war with Iran, the fundamentals of the U.S. economy remain in "good shape." Daly stated, "We are seeing consumers still spending and businesses still investing." She added, "There are indeed concerns that this could push up inflation—that's our responsibility, and we will watch that. There are also concerns that the labor market may not be robust enough, but we haven't seen that; what we are seeing is that it is stabilizing at a good level." (Jinshi)

data.title: Cloudflare Plans Post-Quantum Security by 2029 Amid Advancements in Quantum Research
data.description: Cloudflare has announced its intention to make its platform fully post-quantum secure by 2029, in response to advancements in quantum research that are shortening expected security timelines. According to NS3.AI, Sharon Goldberg stated that Cloudflare aims to initiate post-quantum authentication for origin connections by mid-2026 and complete deployment across all services by 2029. The company highlighted that the same cryptographic mathematics are used to secure both internet authentication and Bitcoin signatures.

data.title: North Sea Oil Demand Surges Amid Tight Supply Conditions
data.description: Traders are actively bidding for oil in the North Sea, indicating ongoing supply constraints. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this activity persists despite a recent drop in futures prices following a ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran. The situation underscores the complexities in the oil market, where geopolitical developments can significantly impact supply and demand dynamics.

data.title: Yuga Labs Settles Lawsuit Over Bored Ape NFT Dispute
data.description: Yuga Labs has reached a settlement in its lawsuit against Ryder Ripps and Jeremy Cahen, concluding a two-year legal battle concerning alleged imitation Bored Ape NFTs. According to NS3.AI, the proposed court orders submitted in a California federal court will permanently prohibit Ripps and Cahen from utilizing Yuga's trademarks and imagery. While the specific terms of the settlement remain undisclosed, the agreement prevents a new trial following an appeals court's decision to overturn a previous award of nearly $9 million to Yuga.

data.title: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu Highlights Nation's Achievements
data.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has emphasized the significant accomplishments achieved by Israel, which once seemed unattainable. According to BlockBeats, Netanyahu stated that there are still numerous goals to be accomplished, and these will be pursued either through advancements or by resuming combat if necessary. He affirmed Israel's readiness to return to battle whenever required.
